# Build Young

> A live, hands-on program for teenagers (ages 15–18) that teaches them to build a real product with AI and grow it into a business. Tagline: "Raising builders, not consumers." Students build something people would pay for, take it live, grow it, and go get their first customers. In an AI world, the edge isn't a degree; it's what you can build.

## About
Build Young is an online program founded by Sunil Garg that teaches teens to build a real product with AI and think like founders. They build a product they believe people would pay for (a small product, app, or service made with AI), take it live, grow it with a funnel and metrics, and go get their first customers. It runs as a 12-week course meeting twice a week (~3 hours/week), delivered live over Zoom. It is hands-on entrepreneurship education; no real money is ever involved.

## Who it's for
Teens ages 15–18 (the "Builders" program). Each season you choose a schedule — Mondays & Wednesdays, or Tuesdays & Thursdays:
- Builders program (ages 15–18) — $999

## What students learn
Act 1 — 0 to 1: build & launch the product (Weeks 1–7):
- Find a real problem people will pay to solve, and write a clear spec
- Build the product with AI (Claude Code) in four layers, one per week: the core product, then accounts & saved data, then payments, then a production-ready pass (emails, findability, safety)
- Go live: a real web address, live payments, and a launch checklist
Act 2 — 1 to 100: grow it into a business (Weeks 8–10):
- Build the funnel into the product: how people find it, try it, and come back — with tracking
- Metrics & scaling: read the real numbers (active users, retention, where people drop off) and pick what to fix
- Product-led growth: a discussion of how the product can grow itself (sharing, referrals)
Act 3 — Go to market (Week 11) + a capstone (Week 12):
- Get your first customers: find who it's for, pitch it, share it where they are, and land your first real users or sales
- A capstone tallying what you built and what it's worth

## Enrolling
Families can book a free 15-minute call with the founder before enrolling. Cancel before the cohort starts for a full refund; after it begins, a prorated refund is available through the end of the first week; non-refundable after that.
